sourceRefs.fallbackText: Robert Griesemer, on behalf of the Go team 1 February 2023
sourceRefs.textQuote: Robert Griesemer, on behalf of the Go team 1 February 2023

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Today the Go team is thrilled to release Go 1.20, which you can get by visiting the download page .
sourceRefs.textQuote: Today the Go team is thrilled to release Go 1.20, which you can get by visiting the download page .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Go 1.20 benefited from an extended development phase, made possible by earlier broad testing and improved overall stability of the code base.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Go 1.20 benefited from an extended development phase, made possible by earlier broad testing and improved overall stability of the code base.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: We’re particularly excited to launch a preview of profile-guided optimization (PGO), which enables the compiler to perform application- and workload-specific optimizations based on run-time profile information. Providing a profile to go build enables the compiler to speed up typical applications by around 3–4%, and we expect future releases to benefit even m
sourceRefs.textQuote: We’re particularly excited to launch a preview of profile-guided optimization (PGO), which enables the compiler to perform application- and workload-specific optimizations based on run-time profile information. Providing

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Go 1.20 also includes a handful of language changes, many improvements to tooling and the library, and better overall performance.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Go 1.20 also includes a handful of language changes, many improvements to tooling and the library, and better overall performance.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The predeclared comparable constraint is now also satisfied by ordinary comparable types , such as interfaces, which will simplify generic code.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The predeclared comparable constraint is now also satisfied by ordinary comparable types , such as interfaces, which will simplify generic code.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The functions SliceData , String , and StringData have been added to package unsafe . They complete the set of functions for implementation-independent slice and string manipulation.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The functions SliceData , String , and StringData have been added to package unsafe . They complete the set of functions for implementation-independent slice and string manipulation.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Go’s type conversion rules have been extended to permit direct conversion from a slice to an array .
sourceRefs.textQuote: Go’s type conversion rules have been extended to permit direct conversion from a slice to an array .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The language specification now defines the exact order in which array elements and struct fields are compared . This clarifies what happens in case of panics during comparisons.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The language specification now defines the exact order in which array elements and struct fields are compared . This clarifies what happens in case of panics during comparisons.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The cover tool now can collect coverage profiles of whole programs, not just of unit tests.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The cover tool now can collect coverage profiles of whole programs, not just of unit tests.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The go tool no longer relies on pre-compiled standard library package archives in the $GOROOT/pkg directory, and they are no longer shipped with the distribution, resulting in smaller downloads. Instead, packages in the standard library are built as needed and cached in the build cache, like other packages.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The go tool no longer relies on pre-compiled standard library package archives in the $GOROOT/pkg directory, and they are no longer shipped with the distribution, resulting in smaller downloads. Instead, packages in the

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The implementation of go test -json has been improved to make it more robust in the presence of stray writes to stdout .
sourceRefs.textQuote: The implementation of go test -json has been improved to make it more robust in the presence of stray writes to stdout .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The go build , go install , and other build-related commands now accept a -pgo flag enabling profile-guided optimizations as well as a -cover flag for whole-program coverage analysis.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The go build , go install , and other build-related commands now accept a -pgo flag enabling profile-guided optimizations as well as a -cover flag for whole-program coverage analysis.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The go command now disables cgo by default on systems without a C toolchain. Consequently, when Go is installed on a system without a C compiler, it will now use pure Go builds for packages in the standard library that optionally use cgo, instead of using pre-distributed package archives (which have been removed, as noted above).
sourceRefs.textQuote: The go command now disables cgo by default on systems without a C toolchain. Consequently, when Go is installed on a system without a C compiler, it will now use pure Go builds for packages in the standard library that o

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The vet tool reports more loop variable reference mistakes that may occur in tests running in parallel.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The vet tool reports more loop variable reference mistakes that may occur in tests running in parallel.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The new crypto/ecdh package provides explicit support for Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman key exchanges over NIST curves and Curve25519.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The new crypto/ecdh package provides explicit support for Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman key exchanges over NIST curves and Curve25519.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The new function errors.Join returns an error wrapping a list of errors which may be obtained again if the error type implements the Unwrap() []error method.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The new function errors.Join returns an error wrapping a list of errors which may be obtained again if the error type implements the Unwrap() []error method.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The new http.ResponseController type provides access to extended per-request functionality not handled by the http.ResponseWriter interface.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The new http.ResponseController type provides access to extended per-request functionality not handled by the http.ResponseWriter interface.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The httputil.ReverseProxy forwarding proxy includes a new Rewrite hook function, superseding the previous Director hook.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The httputil.ReverseProxy forwarding proxy includes a new Rewrite hook function, superseding the previous Director hook.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The new context.WithCancelCause function provides a way to cancel a context with a given error. That error can be retrieved by calling the new context.Cause function.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The new context.WithCancelCause function provides a way to cancel a context with a given error. That error can be retrieved by calling the new context.Cause function.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The new os/exec.Cmd fields Cancel and WaitDelay specify the behavior of the Cmd when its associated Context is canceled or its process exits.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The new os/exec.Cmd fields Cancel and WaitDelay specify the behavior of the Cmd when its associated Context is canceled or its process exits.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Compiler and garbage collector improvements have reduced memory overhead and improved overall CPU performance by up to 2%.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Compiler and garbage collector improvements have reduced memory overhead and improved overall CPU performance by up to 2%.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Work specifically targeting compilation times led to build improvements by up to 10%. This brings build speeds back in line with Go 1.17.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Work specifically targeting compilation times led to build improvements by up to 10%. This brings build speeds back in line with Go 1.17.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: When building a Go release from source , Go 1.20 requires a Go 1.17.13 or newer release. In the future, we plan to move the bootstrap toolchain forward approximately once a year. Also, starting with Go 1.21, some older operating systems will no longer be supported: this includes Windows 7, 8, Server 2008 and Server 2012, macOS 10.13 High Sierra, and 10.14 Mo
sourceRefs.textQuote: When building a Go release from source , Go 1.20 requires a Go 1.17.13 or newer release. In the future, we plan to move the bootstrap toolchain forward approximately once a year. Also, starting with Go 1.21, some older o

sourceRefs.fallbackText: For a complete and more detailed list of all changes see the full release notes .
sourceRefs.textQuote: For a complete and more detailed list of all changes see the full release notes .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Thanks to everyone who contributed to this release by writing code, filing bugs, sharing feedback, and testing the release candidates. Your efforts helped to ensure that Go 1.20 is as stable as possible. As always, if you notice any problems, please file an issue .
sourceRefs.textQuote: Thanks to everyone who contributed to this release by writing code, filing bugs, sharing feedback, and testing the release candidates. Your efforts helped to ensure that Go 1.20 is as stable as possible. As always, if yo
